title: New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter

seo.title: New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter Recipe - Easy & Flavorful
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of cooking New York Strip Steak with garlic butter! This easy recipe delivers a tender, juicy steak bursting with flavor. Perfect for dinner.

seo.imageAlt: Perfectly cooked New York Strip Steak with melted garlic butter on top, garnished with fresh herbs.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in a restaurant-quality New York Strip Steak, pan-seared to perfection and basted with rich garlic herb butter.

description: Savor the rich, beefy flavor of a perfectly cooked New York Strip Steak, enhanced by a luscious garlic herb butter. This American classic is seared to a beautiful crust and basted to juicy perfection, making for an unforgettable meal. It's surprisingly easy to achieve restaurant-quality results right in your own kitchen.

ingredients.notes: or other high smoke point oil

instructions.title: Prepare the Steaks
instructions.text: Remove the New York Strip steaks from the refrigerator 30-45 minutes before cooking to bring them closer to room temperature. Pat them thoroughly dry with paper towels. Season generously all over with kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper.

instructions.title: Heat the Skillet
instructions.text: Place a heavy-bottomed skillet, preferably cast iron, over high heat. Add the avocado oil and heat until it shimmers and just begins to smoke. This indicates the pan is hot enough for a good sear.

instructions.title: Sear the Steaks
instructions.text: Carefully place the seasoned steaks in the hot skillet. Sear undisturbed for 3-4 minutes per side until a deep, golden-brown crust forms. If cooking more than two steaks, sear them in batches to avoid overcrowding the pan and steaming the meat.

instructions.title: Add Aromatics and Butter
instructions.text: Reduce the heat to medium. Add the cold unsalted butter, smashed garlic cloves, rosemary, and thyme sprigs to the skillet. The butter will melt and foam around the steaks, infusing with the aromatics.

instructions.title: Baste the Steaks
instructions.text: Tilt the pan slightly and, using a large spoon, continuously baste the melted garlic herb butter over the steaks for 2-4 minutes, or until they reach your desired internal temperature. For medium-rare, aim for 125-130°F (52-54°C). For medium, aim for 130-135°F (54-57°C).

instructions.title: Rest the Steaks
instructions.text: Remove the steaks from the skillet and transfer them to a cutting board. Tent loosely with foil and let them rest for at least 10 minutes. This crucial step allows the juices to redistribute throughout the meat, ensuring a tender and juicy steak.

instructions.title: Slice and Serve
instructions.text: After resting, slice the New York Strip steaks against the grain into thick pieces. Drizzle with any remaining garlic butter from the pan or cutting board. Serve immediately with your favorite sides.

tags: New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter
tips: Always pat your steaks very dry before searing. Excess moisture prevents a good crust from forming.
For the best flavor, use good quality, unsalted butter and fresh herbs like rosemary and thyme. Adjust garlic to your preference.
Allowing the steak to rest after cooking is non-negotiable for a juicy result. Don't skip this step!
article: New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter is a beloved American Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across American. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
